channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Alibaba Sues Pentagon Over China Military Label
item.description: The Chinese tech giant said it had no ties to China’s military and that the U.S. government had violated the law by making that claim.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Alibaba has become one of China’s leading artificial intelligence companies.

item.title: Trump dice que unos vándalos dañaron el estanque reflectante. Unos documentos internos cuentan otra historia
item.description: Los trabajadores del Servicio de Parques Nacionales encontraron cortes en secciones de espuma, pero no estaban relacionados con el revestimiento ni con las algas.
item.dc:creator: Maxine Joselow and David A. Fahrenthold

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El estanque reflectante, visto desde el Monumento a Washington, con trabajadores intentando controlar las algas el domingo.

item.title: El trabajo remoto ha ayudado a toda una generación de padres y madres
item.description: Hay indicios de que un entorno laboral más flexible y orientado a la familia ha beneficiado a las personas con hijos, a las que cuidan de padres mayores y, sobre todo, a las madres.
item.dc:creator: Claire Cain Miller

media:content.media:credit: Brad Trone para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Elizabeth Terhune, con sus hijos en Santa Fe, Nuevo México, trabajó desde casa cuando nació su hijo menor durante la pandemia. El teletrabajo ha permitido que más madres con hijos pequeños sigan formando parte de la población activa.

item.title: Shirley Lord, Beauty Editor, Novelist and Society Fixture, Dies at 93
item.description: An early champion of the mind-body connection, she held influential positions at Vogue, wrote steamy novels and regularly appeared in the tabloids.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Kaufman

media:content.media:credit: John Lamparski/WireImage, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Shirley Lord in 2016. Her stints at Vogue made her one of the most consequential figures in the beauty industry.

item.title: The Pursuit of Immortality in The Times
item.description: William McDonald, who recently retired as the obituaries editor after nearly two decades, shares how subjects are chosen to be remembered in the newspaper.
item.dc:creator: William McDonald

item.title: Pregnant and Running for Re-election, a Democrat Faces U.S. Prosecution
item.description: Representative LaMonica McIver was charged with assault after an altercation with immigration agents that resulted in no injuries. She faces up to 17 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative LaMonica McIver.

item.title: Dianna Russini Was an N.F.L. ‘Insider.’ Was She Also Out of Bounds?
item.description: Ms. Russini’s closeness to a coach, Mike Vrabel of the New England Patriots, led to her breakup with The Athletic and its parent, The New York Times Company.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Rosman and Ken Belson

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 24, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Exonerated New Yorkers Say Compensation Process Is Traumatic
item.description: They can receive millions of dollars from cities and the state. But sometimes, they must first go on trial again.
item.dc:creator: Willow Higgins and Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: James Irons spent a majority of his life in prison after falsely confessing to a murder. He found himself back in a courtroom to win a lawsuit against the state of New York for his wrongful conviction.

item.title: Asia Tech Shares Swing Wildly as A.I. Jitters Persist
item.description: The turbulence in Asia’s chip-dominated stock markets highlighted how heavily global equities have come to depend on enthusiasm for artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ahn Young-Joon/Associated Press

item.title: China Detained 2 Japanese on Suspicion of Smuggling
item.description: The Japanese nationals were detained in May in the northeastern Chinese city of Dalian, Japan’s government said, amid tensions between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno

media:content.media:credit: Ng Han Guan/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Chinese security at the entrance to the Japanese Embassy in Beijing last year. Tensions between the two countries have been rising.

item.title: Word of the Day: dilatory
item.description: This word has appeared in one article on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Three Parties, Two Boroughs, One Message: Mamdani’s Gamble Pays Off
item.description: From Brooklyn to Manhattan, Mayor Zohran Mamdani made stops at primary victory parties for the three congressional candidates on his slate.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani made appearances at three victory parties on Tuesday, the last with Darializa Avila Chevalier.

item.title: Josh Johnson Muses on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: “Hey, kids, remember you wanted to go to Disneyland? Instead, we’re going to go see the world’s largest kombucha!” Johnson said of the pool renovation as a tourist stop.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Insurgency Over Incumbency: 7 Takeaways From New York’s Primaries
item.description: All three congressional candidates who were backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani won in Tuesday’s primary, bolstering his political movement.
item.dc:creator: Maya King, Sally Goldenberg and Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Bing Guan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani celebrated the victory of state Assemblywoman Claire Valdez, right, in a Democratic primary for a congressional seat.

item.title: El fiasco de Trump con Irán podría salvar a EE. UU. de la próxima guerra
item.description: El fracaso militar y político del conflicto deja un legado inesperado: una aversión duradera a la guerra que podría abrir paso, por fin, a una diplomacia seria.
item.dc:creator: Robert Malley and Stephen Wertheim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración de Sam Whitney/The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Support Is Israel’s True Weakness
item.description: Israel’s dependence on the United States has allowed it to ignore the need for diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Yonatan Touval
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Mamdani Emerges as Kingmaker, Pushing His Slate to a Primary Sweep
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani shook the Democratic establishment by helping drive three progressive candidates to victory.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Brad Lander, center right, was one of the three congressional candidates backed by Mayor Zohran Mandani who won their primaries on Tuesday.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Vance Balancing Disruptive Boss and a Wily Rival
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, June 24, 2026

item.title: With a Turkish ‘Abrazo,’ Istanbul Ascends as a Tango Capital
item.description: Istanbul’s many tango schools, clubs and skilled dancers have won the city recognition, even among Argentine maestros, as a global tango destination.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard, Safak Timur and Laura Boushnak

item.title: Myanmar’s Civil War Has Become an Apocalypse
item.description: Overshadowed by conflicts in Ukraine, Gaza and Lebanon, the fighting in Myanmar’s isolated heartland has reached new depths.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Daniel Berehulak
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Berehulak/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: On a forgotten frontline, the rebels fight deep in the jungle.

item.title: What to Know About Myanmar’s Chaotic War
item.description: The Southeast Asian nation recently experienced its highest monthly civilian death toll since the war began in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Berehulak/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A rebel soldier at a frontline position in the Anyar region of Myanmar.

item.title: On the Front Lines of Myanmar’s Forgotten War
item.description: Our Times correspondent, Hannah Beech, travels to the center of the armed resistance movement in Myanmar and meets rebel fighters who are outgunned and undermanned after years of fighting in a civil war.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Daniel Berehulak, Nailah Morgan and Jiwoong Hong

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Berehulak/The New York Times

item.title: McClain Delaney Wins Democratic House Primary in Maryland Against Trone
item.description: Representative April McClain Delaney defeated David Trone, who lent himself $25 million in his unsuccessful bid to oust the woman who had succeeded him in the House.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Leigh Vogel/Getty Images for MISTR, Free Online PrEP
media:content.media:description: Representative April McClain Delaney, a lawyer and former official in the Biden administration, will be favored in the general election in her Democratic-leaning district.

item.title: Schlossberg’s Defeat Dampens Dream of a Renewed Camelot
item.description: Jack Schlossberg fell well short of his bid to win a highly contested Democratic primary in Manhattan, his campaign hurt by staff defections and missed meetings.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley and Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jack Schlossberg, with his father, Edwin Schlossberg, right, saw his early polling lead evaporate.

media:content.media:credit: Miles G. Cohen for The New York Times

item.title: Who Is Darializa Avila Chevalier?
item.description: Ms. Avila Chevalier pulled off one of the biggest upsets in a New York House primary since Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez defeated Joseph Crowley in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani gave strong support to Darializa Avila Chevalier in her challenge to the Democratic incumbent.

item.title: Darializa Avila Chevalier, a Mamdani Ally, Ousts Espaillat in Primary
item.description: The 32-year-old activist built a robust ground game powered by support from Mayor Zohran Mamdani. She would double the number of democratic socialists in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Maya King

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Darializa Avila Chevalier, a 32-year-old activist, unseated a veteran congressman with Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s support.

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lexi Parra/The New York Times

item.title: Trump-Backed Sticker Magnate Wins House Primary in New York
item.description: Anthony Constantino defeated Assemblyman Robert Smullen, the state Republican Party’s chosen candidate, in a right-leaning upstate district.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Adrianna Newell for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Anthony Constantino had the endorsement of President Trump.

item.title: Hard-Liner Wins G.O.P. Nomination for Governor in Maryland
item.description: The Republican primary victory of Dan Cox sets up a rematch with Wes Moore, who defeated Mr. Cox handily four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson

media:content.media:credit: Steve Ruark/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Dan Cox, the Republican gubernatorial candidate in Maryland, at a campaign event in 2022 when he previously ran for the office.

media:content.media:credit: Miles G. Cohen for The New York Times

item.title: Moderate Democrat Wins Primary in Utah for Newly Blue Salt Lake City Seat
item.description: Ben McAdams, a former representative who had to distance himself from more conservative positions he once held, will be favored to win a blue seat created by court-ordered redistricting.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Schoenbaum/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Former representative Ben McAdams is running to represent Utah’s new Democratic-leaning congressional district.

item.title: Fired Navy Admiral Wins Democratic Runoff in South Carolina’s 1st District
item.description: Democrats expect Nancy Lacore to run a competitive general election despite the district’s Republican leaning.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

item.title: Micah Lasher Wins Manhattan Primary, Defeating Kennedy Heir and Others
item.description: Mr. Lasher, the chosen successor of Representative Jerrold Nadler, beat a field of Democratic candidates that included a fellow state assemblyman in a race for a coveted open seat.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Anna Connors for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Micah Lasher won the Democratic primary for a House seat in District 12 in Manhattan on Tuesday.

item.title: Local Republican Wins Primary for Nancy Mace’s House Seat in South Carolina
item.description: Jenny Costa Honeycutt, a Charleston County councilwoman and lawyer, topped a Republican rival in a runoff primary election for a seat that leans to the right but could be competitive in November.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 24, 2026
item.description: Two can play at Kevin Curry and Zhou Zhang’s crossword.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Peter Stevenson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Remember: The point is to have fun.

media:content.media:credit: Miles G. Cohen for The New York Times

item.title: Cait Conley Wins Primary to Face Mike Lawler in N.Y. Swing District
item.description: Flipping the Hudson Valley seat in November would be an important victory for Democrats as they seek to take back control of the House.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cait Conley won the Democratic nomination for a New York House seat in the Hudson Valley.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Claire Valdez
item.description: Ms. Valdez, a relatively new state lawmaker in New York, won her House primary after a boost from Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who had recruited her to run.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Claire Valdez was one of three candidates endorsed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who has sought to push elevate leftist candidates to Congress.

item.title: Trump’s Pick for Top I.R.S. Lawyer Works at Firm That Represents Him
item.description: James R. Gadwood, the president’s nominee for chief counsel at the Internal Revenue Service, works at Miller & Chevalier, which has represented Mr. Trump in tax matters.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Internal Revenue Service building in Washington. James R. Gadwood is the second person President Trump has nominated to the agency’s top legal job in his second term.

item.title: Ex-Hoyer Aide Buoyed by Super PACs Wins Democratic House Primary in Maryland
item.description: Adrian Boafo, a state delegate, won a primary to succeed the retiring Representative Steny Hoyer with help from more than $11 million in spending from pro-Israel and cryptocurrency interests.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Gail Burton/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Adrian Boafo’s victory in the Democratic primary means he is very likely to win a seat in Congress in November, given how overwhelmingly the seat leans to the left.

item.title: Claire Valdez, a Socialist Backed by Mamdani, Wins Key New York Primary
item.description: Ms. Valdez, a first-term state lawmaker, defeated the Brooklyn borough president, Antonio Reynoso, to win an open House seat in a district divided along demographic fault lines.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Claire Valdez, a state assemblywoman, won an early endorsement from Mayor Zohran Mamdani that helped her overcome some of the institutional support that went to Antonio Reynoso.

item.title: Thomas DiNapoli Beats 2 Challengers in N.Y. Comptroller Primary
item.description: Mr. DiNapoli, who was challenged from the left by Raj Goyle and Drew Warshaw, is on track to become the longest serving comptroller in New York history.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Thomas DiNapoli has served as New York State comptroller since 2007.

item.title: Trump puede usar la expulsión acelerada en todo EE. UU., confirma un tribunal de apelaciones
item.description: Un fallo dividido permite al gobierno estadounidense aplicar el proceso de deportación sin audiencia migratoria a inmigrantes detenidos, sin importar cuánto tiempo lleven viviendo en el territorio.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague

media:content.media:credit: Justin Hamel para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Personas a las que escoltan hacia un vuelo de deportación en El Paso, Texas, en 2023.

item.title: Brad Lander Wins N.Y. House Primary in Which Israel Became a Top Issue
item.description: Mr. Lander, an ally of Mayor Zohran Mamdani, beat Representative Dan Goldman, a two-term incumbent Democrat, in a Lower Manhattan and Brooklyn district.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack

media:content.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Brad Lander won the Democratic primary for a House seat in District 10 in New York.

item.title: A Giraffe Named Gracie Is Missing in Texas
item.description: The giraffe wandered off a private ranch more than a week ago. Her owner is offering a $5,000 reward for information leading to her return.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:credit: Real County Animal Rescue-Shelter
media:content.media:description: The Cedar Hollow Ranch in Leakey, Texas, is missing a giraffe named Gracie.

item.title: Polling in New York’s 12th District Points to a Competitive Race
item.description: Micah Lasher and Alex Bores have consistently led in recent polls as Jack Schlossberg’s support has dipped.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Micah Lasher, left, and Alex Bores at a forum in Manhattan in March.

item.title: In New York’s Seventh Congressional District, Demographics Are Fueling a Competitive Race
item.description: A Democratic primary featuring Antonio Reynoso, Claire Valdez and Julie Won could be defined by the recent influx of young white voters to the district.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler and Emma Schartz

media:content.media:credit: Adam Gray and Ryan Murphy for The New York Times

item.title: Here Are 8 New York State Legislative Races to Watch
item.description: The left and moderate wings of the Democratic Party are fighting it out in several races around New York City.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays and Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Supporters, volunteers and members of the New York City Democratic Socialists of America at a joint campaign rally earlier this month for the candidates they are backing in Assembly races.
